romania.title: Titularizare 2026: Ministerul Educației a publicat subiectele și baremele de la proba scrisă. Când se afișează rezultatele
romania.summary: Examenul începe la ora 9:00 și se desfășoară simultan în 117 centre de concurs din întreaga țară. Primele rezultate vor fi afișate pe 28 iulie, iar rezultatele finale vor fi publicate pe 4 august.

romania.title: 😂 Sportul renaşte! Naţionala de cocalari a României s-a calificat la Insula Mondială a Iubirii din 2028
romania.summary: Chiar dacă steaua fotbalistică a ţării noastre a apus, sunt alte domenii în care strălucim! Echipa naţională de cocalari şi başoalde a României tocmai s-a calificat matematic la Insula Mondială a Iubirii 2028, după ce s-a impus în faţa gherţoilor şi matracucilor din Macedonia de Nord cu scorul categoric de 69 la 43. „Hăhă, 69. romania.summary: Deşi nimeni nu prea l-a băgat în seamă în cei patru ani de liceu, tânărul Aureliu Poplică din Brăila s-a trezit brusc vedeta generaţiei sale! Wow!” appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 Ca să reducă factura la curent, un român și-a trecut mașina de spălat pe GPL
romania.summary: Marian Coltea, un cetățean din județul Argeș a găsit o soluție inedită pentru reducerea facturii la curent electric, convertindu-și mașina de spălat rufe pentru a funcționa pe GPL. Bărbatul a instalat un sistem de alimentare cu gaz petrolier lichefiat, susținând că a reușit astfel să scadă semnificativ costurile lunare cu energia electrică. Potrivit declarațiilor sale, […] The post Ca să reducă factura la curent, un român și-a trecut mașina de spălat pe GPL appeared first on Times New Roman .

medical.title: How to keep babies safe when temperatures soar
medical.summary: Infants, toddlers and young children need outdoor activity to build strong bodies and minds, but they also require extra care in the heat.

medical.title: Last-line antibiotics at risk as children's resistance rises
medical.summary: Antimicrobial resistance in children is rising globally and will worsen over the next decade, threatening the effectiveness of life-saving antibiotics, according to the world's first monitoring platform. The study, led by Murdoch Children's Research Institute (MCRI) in collaboration with the University of Sydney, Clinton Health Access Initiative (CHAI) and The Chinese University of Hong Kong, analyzed more than 106,000 infection samples from children aged up to 18 years across 82 countries.

medical.title: Researchers analyzing colon cancer screenings find hope for higher retest rates
medical.summary: Nearly half of patients who completed an at-home test for colorectal cancer fail to repeat the screening consistently, according to Rutgers Health researchers, who also found primary care provider behaviors can increase patient consistency—and catch more cancers early.

medical.title: Researchers explain differences in brain tissue vulnerability in neurological disease
medical.summary: Many neurological diseases damage specific parts of the brain, even though the harmful protein involved is present throughout the brain. Researchers at Texas Children's Duncan Neurological Research Institute (Duncan NRI) and Baylor College of Medicine report new evidence that explains these differences in brain tissue vulnerability in a study published in Genes & Development.

medical.title: When it comes to brain health, is microbiome science missing much of the world?
medical.summary: For more than two decades, scientists have uncovered remarkable links between the trillions of microbes living in our gut and the health of our brains. This research has transformed our understanding of stress, depression, anxiety and neurodevelopment, opening new possibilities for preventing and treating mental illness. What if much of this science is based on studying only a narrow slice of global diversity?

medical.title: Largest genetic analysis of borderline personality disorder identifies 11 risk regions and nine genes
medical.summary: An international team led by the Central Institute of Mental Health in Mannheim has presented the largest genome-wide association study on borderline personality disorder to date. Data from about 13,000 affected people and more than 1.1 million controls from 14 countries were analyzed. The study identified 11 independent risk regions and nine genes that could contribute to the development of the disorder. The findings, published in Nature Genetics, show clear genetic overlaps with other mental and physical illnesses and open new perspectives for research and care.

medical.title: Researchers study barriers to GLP-1 insurance coverage
medical.summary: Despite growing evidence that GLP-1 medications improve health and are worth their cost, many insurance plans still limit coverage. University of Mississippi researchers are examining why and where that leaves patients.

medical.title: Keeping drugs free of contaminants—pharmaceutical manufacturers filter medications through tiny pores
medical.summary: Every year, the global pharmaceutical industry produces trillions of doses of medications. Manufacturing these drugs involves several stages, and the chance of contamination increases with every step.

medical.title: Green neighborhoods may help reduce long-term stress during pregnancy, new study finds
medical.summary: Pregnant women who live in greener neighborhoods may have lower levels of long-term stress, according to a new study led by researchers from Bar-Ilan University. The study found that greater exposure to residential greenery during pregnancy was associated with lower levels of cortisol and cortisone—two key stress hormones that can affect both maternal and fetal health.

science.title: 30,000 people tried for bigamy over the past 400 years in England and Wales, archives reveal
science.summary: Bigamy has been a rare crime during the past 400 years—and offenders have been harshly punished. It seems likely that no more than 30,000 individuals have ever been tried for bigamy in England and Wales during this period. Most have been convicted, and possible punishment has ranged from execution, branding, whipping, transportation and imprisonment to fines and community service in more recent decades.

science.title: Molecules stop carbon nanotubes clumping, unlocking record heat-to-electricity performance
science.summary: QUT researchers have overcome a challenge that has limited next-generation energy-harvesting materials for more than two decades, opening the door to more powerful wearable electronics and new ways of turning wasted heat into electricity. The breakthrough centers on carbon nanotubes, which are flexible, conductive microscopic rods that have long shown promise for wearable technologies but have been difficult to control.

science.title: Carbon nanotube model reveals how smart sensors perform at aircraft-like extremes
science.summary: Researchers from Skoltech, in collaboration with the Harbin Institute of Technology and Jiangsu University (China), have published a study presenting the first empirical model to explain and predict the sensing behavior of hierarchical tri-phase carbon nanotube systems across a wide temperature range (from -170°C (-274°F) to 90°C (194°F)). The study has been published in the Carbon journal.

science.title: Early career funding improves scientific influence and citations for women
science.summary: Early-career funding has a bigger impact on women than men when it comes to their careers in science, according to a new study from City St George's, University of London. While the study found that successful applicants in both groups secure more future research funding than unsuccessful applicants, it also found that successful female applicants receive more citations and are cited more often in their field than female applicants who did not receive funding.

science.title: Ancestral variation, not new mutations, may impact survival of species facing climate change
science.summary: Research co-authored by an Oklahoma Museum of Natural History curator, her graduate student and an international team of scientists has shed new light on the evolutionary "toolkit" animals use to survive environmental change.

science.title: NASA Sets Briefings for SpaceX Crew-13 Mission to Space Station
science.summary: NASA and its partners will discuss the upcoming crew rotation mission to the International Space Station during a pair of news conferences on Monday, Aug. 3, from the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston. Mission leadership will provide an overview of NASA’s SpaceX Crew‑13 mission at 12 p.m. EDT. Next, crew members will discuss their […]

science.title: NASA, GE Aerospace Work Enables Hybrid-Electric Flight Demonstration
science.summary: An aircraft powered by a megawatt-class hybrid-electric engine developed in collaboration with NASA and built by GE Aerospace, demonstrated flight of an innovation that can inform new generations of fuel-saving aircraft power systems. Mounted to a Saab 340B aircraft, the engine flew at Farnborough International Air Show in the United Kingdom. It was the public […]

science.title: New NASA Earth Missions Gear Up to Start Science Flights
science.summary: The first of six new airborne campaigns will hit the skies this summer.

environment.title: Bornean ferret badger only lives in Borneo. Could it be a conservation symbol?
environment.summary: The Bornean ferret badger is a small carnivore with the slinky body of a ferret and a face mask like a badger. A new study confirms that it lives only in the mountains of Sabah, a Malaysian state on the island of Borneo. Ferret badgers are nocturnal carnivores, widespread across Southeast Asia, but the Bornean […]

environment.title: Mozambique completes first white rhino breeding population in decades
environment.summary: On June 6, nine female white rhinos arrived in Mozambique’s Zinave National Park following a two-day translocation. Their arrival marks the culmination of nearly 10 years of rhino reintroduction efforts in the park, aimed at rebuilding a viable breeding population of the mammals in Zinave after decades of local extinction. The white rhinos (Ceratotherium simum) […]

environment.title: ‘Flamingo Revolution’ aims to stop Kushner-backed resort on protected Albanian delta
environment.summary: VJOSA-NARTA, Albania — In late April, heavy machinery began moving into the Pishë Poro-Narta protected landscape on Albania’s Adriatic coast without permits or public notice. Bulldozers and excavators felled coastal pine trees, flattened sand dunes, and cut new roads through previously untouched habitat. Then, barbed wire fences went up along the shoreline. The incursion was […]

environment.title: The secret underground system keeping the Grand Canyon alive
environment.summary: Scientists are venturing into the Grand Canyon’s hidden cave networks to solve a mystery: how snowmelt travels underground to supply the park’s vital springs. Their discoveries could help protect the canyon’s water from drought, contamination, and other growing threats.

environment.title: DNA solves 250-year-old mystery of the Seychelles’ lost crocodiles
environment.summary: Scientists have solved the mystery of the Seychelles’ vanished crocodiles using DNA from historic museum specimens. The reptiles were not a unique species after all, but an isolated population of saltwater crocodiles that likely drifted thousands of kilometers across the Indian Ocean.

environment.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
environment.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds. The research suggests these wrens are evolving independently, developing unique songs, appearances, and genetics that may eventually turn them into entirely new species.

environment.title: A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground
environment.summary: A casual walk through an Ithaca cemetery led to the discovery of a gigantic hidden bee population — roughly 5.5 million ground-nesting bees packed beneath the soil. Scientists believe it may be one of the largest bee aggregations ever documented and say the insects are crucial pollinators for apple orchards and other crops. The bees have likely lived there for more than 100 years, thriving in the cemetery’s undisturbed sandy soil.

environment.title: Earth’s orbital wobble triggered rapid climate chaos during the dinosaur age
environment.summary: New research suggests Earth’s climate can swing wildly on surprisingly short timescales — even during hot, ice-free greenhouse periods. By studying ancient sediments from the Late Cretaceous, scientists uncovered repeating climate shifts tied to tiny changes in Earth’s orbital wobble. These cycles may have repeatedly pushed the planet between humid and arid states every few thousand years.

joke_ro.text: Ce faci diseara? - Ma odihnesc de la cat n-am facut azi.

satire_ro.title: Sportul renaşte! Naţionala de cocalari a României s-a calificat la Insula Mondială a Iubirii din 2028
